task.title: Restore missing separator spaces in formatter output
task.summary: The formatter now handles extra whitespace noise and reserved statement-head boundaries much better, but it still preserves or emits some shapes where required separator spaces have been removed.
task.problem: A small new batch of fixture edits shows the opposite failure mode from the earlier whitespace-collapse work: tokens that should be visually separated remain jammed together after formatting. Current examples include typed parameter forms like `instance_name?:string`, closure signatures like `where {|entry|...}`, brace-adjacent record or error forms like `msg: ...\"}`, and pipeline/call boundaries like `|save --force ...`. These are formatter-scope spacing decisions, not semantic reconstruction.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Typed parameter and binding shapes restore the formatter's normal separator spacing around `:` and `?` boundaries where the syntax expects them.
Closure signatures and brace-adjacent forms restore the house spacing around `{`, `|`, and `}` instead of preserving jammed-together source text.
Pipeline and command boundaries restore missing spaces, including forms like `| save` rather than `|save`.
Regression tests are drawn from the real fixture residue in `lima-ports.nu` and `download-latest-iso.nu`, not only from synthetic one-liners.
